fault_hypothesis: Lines 71-72 incorrectly remove the last element when both lists share the same last value, but a merge should never remove duplicates — this post-processing block is entirely wrong and causes one element to be dropped.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the merged list length must still equal the sum of both input lengths — the pop violates this invariant.
The then-branch of the condition at line 71 should never be entered because removing a duplicate last element is incorrect behavior.
After initialization, merged must be empty before any elements are appended.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
